definition: A set of voluntary standards for the certification of agricultural products around the globe. The standard is primarily designed to reassure consumers about how food is produced on the farm by minimizing detrimental environmental impacts of farming operations, reducing the use of chemical inputs and ensuring a responsible approach to worker health and safety, as well as animal welfare.

A standard that connects physical products to the web through a single, standardized web link, typically encoded in a QR code. This allows consumers and supply chain partners to access a variety of online information and services related to the product.
A unique 13-digit identification number assigned to every producer or producer group registered in the GlobalG.A.P. certification system, used for verifying certification status.

A comprehensive standard from GlobalG.A.P. that covers all stages of production, from pre-harvest activities such as soil management and plant protection product application to post-harvest handling, packaging and storage.
A unique and internationally recognized identifier for a product, used to look up product information in a database.
